subject: [PATCH v2 031/110] hfs: use PRIino format for i_ino

reviews.author: Jeff Layton (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged that the PRIino format is used for i_ino in the patch, and it's a preparation for widening of i_ino via kino_t.

reviews.author: Viacheslav Dubeyko
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that the PRIino format specifier is missing a space between the % and the field name, which can lead to incorrect formatting of inode numbers in debug messages.
reviews.raw_body:
Reviewed-by: Viacheslav Dubeyko <slava@dubeyko.com>

Thanks,
Slava.
______________________________________________________
Linux MTD discussion mailing list
http://lists.infradead.org/mailman/listinfo/linux-mtd/

2026-03-02.patch_summary: This patch updates the HFS file system to use the PRIino format for printing inode numbers, in preparation for the future widening of inode values from 32-bit to 64-bit via the kino_t type.
